query: Show me references to respiratory illness, unusual pneumonia, and Wuhan across 2019–2020 archive content — surface the earliest mentions and how the coverage evolved.

payload.entity: Respiratory illness / unusual pneumonia / Wuhan outbreak coverage in the 2019–2020 archive
payload.query: Show me references to respiratory illness, unusual pneumonia, and Wuhan across 2019–2020 archive content — surface the earliest mentions and how the coverage evolved.

timeline.dominant_theme: Earliest disclosure — “pneumonia of unknown cause” in Wuhan

representative_clip.title: CCTV 新闻直播间 — Wuhan reports pneumonia of unknown cause
representative_clip.reason: China's state broadcaster CCTV reports “不明原因肺炎” (pneumonia of unknown cause) in Wuhan on Dec 31, 2019 — weeks before any “coronavirus” or “COVID-19” terminology existed.

scenes.title: CCTV 新闻直播间 — Wuhan reports pneumonia of unknown cause
scenes.reason: China's state broadcaster CCTV reports “不明原因肺炎” (pneumonia of unknown cause) in Wuhan on Dec 31, 2019 — weeks before any “coronavirus” or “COVID-19” terminology existed.

representative_clip.title: Arirang report on mysterious pneumonia outbreak in Wuhan
representative_clip.reason: The anchor explicitly calls it 'a mysterious pneumonia outbreak in the Chinese city of Wuhan,' a clear pre-COVID-19 framing.

scenes.title: Arirang report on mysterious pneumonia outbreak in Wuhan
scenes.reason: The anchor explicitly calls it 'a mysterious pneumonia outbreak in the Chinese city of Wuhan,' a clear pre-COVID-19 framing.

scenes.title: Arirang segment titled 'Wuhan pneumonia spreads further'
scenes.reason: On-screen graphics read 'WUHAN PNEUMONIA SPREADS FURTHER,' directly showing pre-name terminology.

scenes.title: South Korea alert over Wuhan pneumonia outbreak
scenes.reason: The report ties pneumonia symptoms in Korea to recent travel from Wuhan, reflecting early cross-border alert coverage before official naming.

scenes.title: Singapore screens travelers from Wuhan over pneumonia outbreak
scenes.reason: The lower-third reads 'CHINA PNEUMONIA OUTBREAK' as Singapore announces temperature screening for travelers from Wuhan.

scenes.title: CNA report on 'Wuhan virus' spread
scenes.reason: The studio graphic says 'WUHAN VIRUS' while the report discusses rapidly rising cases and spread beyond China.

scenes.title: WHO stops short of emergency declaration report
scenes.reason: This report shows the late-January WHO emergency debate, when the story was still framed as a coronavirus outbreak rather than a named disease.

representative_clip.title: WHO announcement naming the disease COVID-19
representative_clip.reason: Tedros explicitly announces and spells 'COVID-19,' marking the transition away from Wuhan- and pneumonia-based labels.

scenes.title: WHO announcement naming the disease COVID-19
scenes.reason: Tedros explicitly announces and spells 'COVID-19,' marking the transition away from Wuhan- and pneumonia-based labels.

representative_clip.title: WHO press briefing declaring COVID-19 a pandemic
representative_clip.reason: Tedros says COVID-19 'can be characterized as a pandemic,' the clearest statement of the narrative turning point.

scenes.title: WHO press briefing declaring COVID-19 a pandemic
scenes.reason: Tedros says COVID-19 'can be characterized as a pandemic,' the clearest statement of the narrative turning point.

scenes.title: ABC coverage of WHO pandemic declaration
scenes.reason: ABC’s version emphasizes 'alarming levels of spread and severity' and frames the declaration as a global escalation.

scenes.title: Explainer package on WHO pandemic declaration
scenes.reason: Another packaged report explains what the word 'pandemic' means and warns against complacency after the declaration.

scenes.title: Short report asking what the pandemic declaration means
scenes.reason: This clip pairs Tedros’s declaration with explanatory framing about the significance of the pandemic label.

representative_clip.title: DW report on UK beginning Pfizer-BioNTech vaccinations
representative_clip.reason: The report announces Britain’s rollout of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine and visually anchors the first public immunization phase.

scenes.title: DW report on UK beginning Pfizer-BioNTech vaccinations
scenes.reason: The report announces Britain’s rollout of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine and visually anchors the first public immunization phase.

scenes.title: ABC News segment on first Americans getting vaccine doses
scenes.reason: ABC’s rollout segment shows vaccine vials, packaging lines, and cautions that vaccination does not yet eliminate transmission risk.

payload.narrative_summary: The archive does not surface direct 2019 clips; the earliest visible references are in Jan 2020, when broadcasters describe a Wuhan-linked 'mystery' or 'pneumonia-like' respiratory illness. Coverage then formalizes in Feb with the WHO naming of COVID-19, globalizes in Mar with the pandemic declaration, and turns cautiously optimistic in Dec with vaccine rollout coverage.

summary_bullets.headline: Before it had a name
summary_bullets.text: China's CCTV reports an unexplained pneumonia in Wuhan on Dec 31, 2019 — the earliest archive trace, weeks before the WHO naming.

summary_bullets.headline: Mystery illness emerges
summary_bullets.text: January coverage centers on a Wuhan-linked unexplained respiratory illness, using labels like 'Wuhan pneumonia,' 'mysterious respiratory virus,' and 'viral pneumonia' before the COVID-19 name existed.

summary_bullets.headline: Official naming replaces local shorthand
summary_bullets.text: In February, WHO’s naming of COVID-19 shifts reporting away from symptom-based and place-based labels toward a standardized global term.

summary_bullets.headline: Outbreak becomes pandemic story
summary_bullets.text: March coverage reframes the crisis as a pandemic, stressing alarming spread, severity, and the consequences of inaction.

summary_bullets.headline: Rollout brings cautious hope
summary_bullets.text: December coverage focuses on first vaccinations, freezer logistics, supply limits, and the message that vaccines help but do not end precautions immediately.

estimated_value.calculation_basis: Proxy estimate based on 14 distinct broadcast clips in the retrieved corpus (incl. the Dec 31 2019 CCTV disclosure), valued at ~$500 per distinct verified mention/scene for historical media-intelligence use.

inflection_points.label: First public disclosure
inflection_points.why: CCTV reports “pneumonia of unknown cause” in Wuhan — the earliest reference in the archive, before any COVID-19 terminology.

inflection_points.label: Earliest mystery-pneumonia framing
inflection_points.why: Coverage first appears as a Wuhan-linked unexplained respiratory illness and 'pneumonia' story, before the disease had an official name.

inflection_points.label: WHO names the disease COVID-19
inflection_points.why: The narrative shifts from geographic and symptom-based labels to a standardized global term designed to avoid stigma.

inflection_points.label: Pandemic declaration
inflection_points.why: Coverage pivots from outbreak tracking to worldwide spread, severity, and urgent government action after WHO’s pandemic declaration.

inflection_points.label: Vaccine rollout begins
inflection_points.why: The story turns from crisis escalation to cautious hope, with first-dose logistics and reminders that masking and distancing still matter.
